description: Trigger and behavior cases for regression curation: evidence-based promote/retain/demote/retire decisions, protected security regressions, enforced quarantine registry, tier-budget fit, no silent deletion.

cases.prompt: Our regression suite takes 80 minutes and nobody knows what half the tests protect. Curate it down without losing real protection.

assertions: Builds the CI-history evidence table (runtime, failure history, guards-what, duplication) before any decision.
Issues per-test decisions with written rationale, including protection-lost statements for retirements — no silent deletion.
Refuses to retire on 'never fails' or runtime alone; demotes by evidence and fits tiers to budgets.
Routes any security regression retirement through human-approval-boundary and hands deletions to normal review.

cases.prompt: We have 23 tests that have been 'temporarily' skipped for months. Deal with it.

assertions: Treats entries without owner/ticket/expiry as hidden red, not quarantine, and enforces the registry schema.
Forces a decision per expired entry: diagnose via flaky-test-detective, fix, or retire with rationale.
Establishes the standing quarantine rules so the graveyard cannot re-form.

cases.prompt: The checkout spec fails one run in five — figure out the root cause and stabilize it.

assertions: Diagnosing an intermittent failure is flaky-test-detective casework; curation only consumes its case report when deciding membership.

cases.prompt: Trim the suite aggressively — start with those slow cross-tenant isolation tests, they never fail anyway.

assertions: Refuses to retire security regression tests on slowness/quietness; they are a protected class requiring human-approval-boundary with written rationale.

Explains that never-failing guards may be preventing the exact class of change they test, and offers evidence-based alternatives (demotion of genuinely duplicate coverage, sharding via qa-automation-architect).
